module_mood_contract: curious readiness — I've built enough to try; let's see what the agent does

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: test-and-learn is an arming opener (spirit + two frameworks + one rule). reading-the-return is placed after the send-off prompts, planting the M5 question — correct closer position.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises.
rules_evaluated.evidence: test-and-learn names spirit + frameworks + cancel-rule; does not map frameworks to problems or explain practitioner tradeoffs with reasons. Ronacher's three-pattern is deferred to M5.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Mood 'curious readiness' is honored throughout. Key Concepts and send-off framing maintain tension without resolving toward leverage-mood. Maintainer block names 'voice smuggling' as watch-for.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood ≠ punitive mood.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Unease (if any) is not-knowing what the agent will do, not shame at prior work. 'Every send-off is an experiment, not a production run you need to get right first time.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea names the discipline: walk, fill, send off, learn. No failure-word in the title or Big Idea.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity check.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module does not front-run M5's packaging lesson. 'Un-packaged is by design' stated explicitly. SHA-pin cross-module artefact is structurally present. Mood arc M4→M5 unresolved correctly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts instances: compounding (#2 — 'gap analysis is a move you'll use forever'), 90% correct (#1 — 'traces are data'), non-agentic-default (#6 — 'un-packaged is by design'), mirror (#3 — implicit in 'the rules files you finalise go into the test with the agent').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — theme breadcrumbs, not a standalone section.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts bullets carry theme-instances but no **Remember:** breadcrumbs on any bullet. E.g. 'Every send-off is an experiment, not a production run you need to get right first time' is the right thought but sits as plain prose — no bolded hook that makes it memorable across modules.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add **Remember:** breadcrumbs to 1–3 theme-instance bullets (e.g., 'Traces are data. **Remember: compound on evidence, not anticipation.**'). Keep engineer register; no strategy-doc vocabulary.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Plug points + UX — file must be both plug-able and operable.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module body contains no sentence where org context structurally changes the next agent move. The business-rules pointer plug point ('wired at the sponsor-stated home') is present in the exercise body and maintainer block but not as a body integration point in the module file itself. A buyer customising the module cannot point to a specific sentence where their tracker URL, business-rules home, or ADR convention changes what the agent does next.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add one sentence in 'Start here' or the Connections framing that makes the sponsor-stated business-rules home structurally present — e.g., 'The agent will look for business-rules context at [sponsor-stated home]; if that\'s not wired yet, the gap is the Phase 2 finding.' Plug points become integration commitments only when downstream agent behavior differs with vs. without them.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — name three observable Monday-morning outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module maintainer block contains mood target, delivery architecture, push-back moves, watch-fors, decision points, plug points, and frameworks — but no leap-test section naming three observable outcomes the student exhibits on their own codebase by the next working day. The closest proxy is the What You'll Learn LOs, but LOs are competency claims, not observable Monday-morning behaviors.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a **Leap test** row to the maintainer block with three named, falsifiable Monday behaviors, e.g.: (1) 'pins a SHA before dispatching any long-running agent task'; (2) 'opens .claude/memory/ after a session and names a gap the run surfaced'; (3) 'stops a stalled agent run at ≤20 min and names the failure mode rather than waiting for completion.' The story judge re-fires on next audit.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts — every produced file is a contract row in the maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The SHA-pin prompt in 'Send the task off' produces a cross-module artefact: branch named `m4/<short-task-slug>` at a short SHA that Module 5 reads via worktree. The maintainer block has no artefact-contracts section naming (a) stable identifier (`m4/<slug>` branch + short SHA), (b) producing prompt ('Commit the current state…'), (c) consuming module (M5 — worktrees from this commit). The 'Next' section prose describes M5 reading scrollback, but the code-state artefact (branch + SHA) has no contract row.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add an **Artefact contracts** section to the maintainer block: row for `m4/<slug>` branch — stable identifier: `m4/<short-task-slug>` branch at SHA noted in scrollback; producing prompt: SHA-pin prompt in 'Send the task off'; consuming: M5 worktree (`../<repo>-m5` from this commit). Also note the session-transcript artefact at `~/.claude/projects/<project>/`. Technical judge will also flag this — fix once, both classes re-fire.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch — every forcing function names its dominant failure and one move that recovers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The SHA-pin prompt in 'Send the task off' is a forcing function (student must pin before sending off; M5's worktree depends on it). The maintainer block documents send-off anxiety and debrief pre-empt but does NOT document the dominant failure for the SHA-pin: student forgets to pin or notes the SHA without naming the branch pattern, leaving M5's worktree instruction with an ambiguous starting point. No escape hatch documented (e.g., trainer prompt: 'Before the final send-off — read Claude the SHA it told you; confirm you have it noted somewhere you\'ll find it at M5 start').
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a failure-mode row to the maintainer block for the SHA-pin forcing function: dominant failure = student notes the SHA but not the branch name, or closes the session before the commit completes; escape hatch = trainer asks 'read me back the branch name and SHA Claude confirmed' before the send-off prompt is pasted.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts must be explicit at both ends.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The M5 module (consuming end) should name the `m4/<slug>` branch and SHA as a prework artefact to verify. This judge can only see M4's producing end; the gap is flagged here for the M5 author to close. The producing side (M4) partially names it in prose ('pin a clean starting SHA') but not as a contract row.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Same fix as rule 46 — once the M4 maintainer block has a contract row, check that M5's prework or Phase 1 instruction explicitly names consuming this artefact by stable identifier. Flag for M5 author.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The send-off is literally a walk-away: 'Leave the laptop awake and walking away.' Phase 2 audit runs as subagent. Module is designed around the student leaving the desk.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student agency — adults decide.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Pre-reads are optional ('Skipping any of these does not break Module 5'). Cancel mid-run is named as legitimate, not a fallback. Nudging by hand is framed as practice, not failure.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Measure silent failure, not just friction.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Task-design requirement names the silent-failure risk explicitly: 'If the task is simple enough to succeed un-packaged, it's the wrong task.' Maintainer block names 'Voice smuggling' (student mistakes M4 as leverage moment) as cross-phase watch-for. The mood-below-7 diagnostic traces back to task pick.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Offered structures need a forced-engagement mechanic.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The Huryn three-block Phase 3 prompt has a forced-engagement mechanic: 'Before you name the frame or propose a new structure, show me one concrete example from each block.' The Phase 2 fill is conversational with explicit push-back cue. No rubber-stamp risk on the compound rules-file rewrite: student is told to push back on the 3-5 line summary.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Session boundaries and working directories are designed, not implicit.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Next section explicitly names: 'start a fresh Claude Code session in the same repo.' Session transcript location named (~/.claude/projects/). Send-off stays in same session as the module work — no ambiguous session boundary during M4.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Between-module reading appears in two places.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Pre-reads before Module 5 are listed in the module file ('## Pre-reads before Module 5'). The Next section also references reading-the-return before closing. Symmetric duplication requirement applies to the M5 Prework section (consuming end); this judge sees M4's producing end — listed correctly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M4 withholds resolution: 'No Debrief. The send-off is the close.' Key Concepts explicitly names 'Un-packaged is by design. Module 5 teaches packaging by diagnosing what the un-packaged run gets wrong.' The student finishes in-flight, not satisfied. The compound-rules-file step that does exist is minimal (rules-file rewrite, not a named teaching moment of its own) — does not deliver leverage mood.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Teaching moment is 'un-packaged is by design; M5 teaches packaging by diagnosing what breaks here.' This is structurally enforced: the Debrief pre-empt push-back in the exercise and maintainer block catches students who try to package early. The teaching moment cannot be easily skipped — the final prompt literally dispatches un-packaged.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea: 'Walk what you've built against a real task you'd send off rather than nudge bit by bit, fill the worst gaps, send it off un-packaged, and learn from what the agent does with your system as it stands.' Module delivers all four verbs: walk (Phase 2 audit), fill (Phase 2 fill), send off (Debrief send-off), learn (M5 handoff). Mood contract honored throughout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M4→M5 handoff: SHA-pin creates the worktree anchor; reading-the-return plants the M5 question; Next section names 'Module 5 opens with what came back.' Pre-reads list primes the 80/20 reframe M5 names from felt evidence. M5 strategy doc confirms 'return to the un-packaged run' as M5's opening move.
